_comment: Hyperparameters for the type-primary forecast pipeline (predict_2026_types.py / forecast_engine.run_forecast).
_tuning_notes: lam and mu were calibrated on 2026-04-01 via scripts/calibrate_lam_mu.py. See data/experiments/lam_mu_sweep_raw.csv and lam_mu_sweep_summary.csv for full results.
_lam_notes: lam=1.0 retained. Calibration (10x10 grid, lam in [0.1,20], mu in [0.1,20]) shows monotonic RMSE improvement with higher lam using historical 3-state validation data (FL/GA/AL only). Improvement is small (~0.0001 RMSE from lam=1 to lam=20) and the trend toward higher lam is a validation-data artifact: sparse 3-state polls add noise rather than signal. With national poll coverage (18 races in production), lower lam is appropriate and the prior trust/poll balance at lam=1.0 is correct. The pure-prior asymptote (lam→∞) achieves RMSE=0.12294 vs lam=1.0 at RMSE=0.12633, confirming polls matter when coverage is national.
_mu_notes: mu=1.0 retained. Calibration confirms mu has essentially zero impact on forecast RMSE across the full [0.1,20] sweep range. This is expected: delta_race is underdetermined when each race has only a handful of state-level polls. mu will matter more once richer within-state poll crosstabs are available (Tier 1 W vectors). Any positive value in [0.1,10] is valid for now.
_w_vector_mode_notes: core vs full modes produce identical results until method_reach_profiles gains entries for phone_live, phone_ivr, and unknown methodologies. See Known Tech Debt in CLAUDE.md.

poll_weighting._half_life_notes: Exponential decay half-life in days. At 30 days a poll loses half its effective weight. Shorter values prioritize recency; longer values retain more historical polling signal. Calibrate via scripts/experiments/sweep_poll_half_life.py.
poll_weighting._pre_primary_discount_notes: Multiplicative factor applied to n_sample for polls conducted before the primary. Pre-primary polls are less informative about general election matchups. Must be in (0, 1]. 0.5 = pre-primary polls count for half.
